title: Atlas CLI — JSON error envelope
description: The single discriminated error object every `brain` command emits on stdout in `--json` mode when it fails (design §CLI, plan §2.5). Discriminated on `code` — a stable, command-specific string enumerated per command in the sibling `<command>.schema.json` files. Each `code` maps to exactly one plan §2.5 process exit category (0 ok · 1 validation · 2 config/vault/lock · 3 secret-scan · 4 internal · 5 user/usage · 6 action-required). `details` carries code-specific structured remediation data (an open object keyed under the common `field`/`path`/`location`) so a consumer never has to parse `message`/`hint`. Batch commands (`jobs run|retry|cancel` with `--all`/multiple targets) use the separate batch-result object, not this envelope — that is the sole exception and is encoded in the jobs command schemas.

code.description: Stable, command-specific discriminator. Enumerated (with its exit category) in the emitting command's `x-atlas-contract.errorCodes`. Composite lock codes take the form `locked:<scope>`; authorization-drift codes take the form `authz.<reason>` (catalog in security-broker-contract.md).

message.description: Human-readable, terminal-safe summary. Never the sole carrier of machine-actionable data — that lives in `details`.

hint.description: Actionable next step for a human operator. May be empty when no remediation applies.

errors.description: Present when a single invocation produced multiple failures (e.g. many validation errors). Each item is a nested envelope of the same shape.

retryable.description: The boolean gate: MAY the caller retry the identical invocation? Independent of `retryAfterMs` (which only carries provider-directed timing when present).

retryAfterMs.description: Provider-directed retry delay in integer milliseconds. Present iff the underlying provider supplied timing (a normalized `rate_limit`/`quota` `retryAfter`); absence does not imply non-retryable — consult `retryable`.

runId.description: Included whenever the failing command operates on a specific run.

jobId.description: Included whenever the failing command operates on a specific job.

location.description: Source location for the failure, when it maps to a concrete file.

details.description: Code-specific typed remediation data. The common keys `field`/`path`/`location` are defined here; any additional keys are the code-specific structured fields documented per error code (e.g. `dependents[]` for `has-dependents`, lock-holder pid/scope/start-time for `locked:<scope>`, `backupWatermarkSeq`/`latestLedgerSeq` for `backup-unhealthy`).

nestedError.description: A single failure inside `errors[]`. Same shape as the top-level envelope, minus its own recursive `errors[]`.

examples.message: Invalid configuration: sqlite.ledger_backup.keep must be a positive integer.
examples.hint: Fix the offending key in brain.config.yaml.

examples.message: The vault-maintenance lock is held by another process.
examples.hint: Wait for the holder to finish — a dead holder's lock is reclaimed automatically on the next acquire.

examples.message: Ledger-writing runs are blocked: the latest verified backup does not cover the current ledger sequence.
examples.hint: Run `brain db backup`, or `brain db backup --force-unblock` to accept the RPO gap (audited).
Fix the reported notes and re-run.

examples.message: The privileged operation requires an OS presence assertion bound to the broker challenge.
examples.hint: Re-run with an interactive presence assertion, or use --export-challenge → sign → --authorization.
